name: DMR Buyer Guide
description: Activate when a user asks buyer-oriented questions about practical suitability, family car selection, towing requirements, seat count needs, inspection implications, or interpreting DMR performance metrics as real-world driving experience.
content: Load this skill when translating DMR statistics into practical buyer guidance. Anchor all advice in tool output — do not make purchase recommendations without data. Use the buyer-thresholds reference for interpreting power-to-weight, seat count, and inspection outcomes in plain language.

referenced_content.name: buyer-thresholds
referenced_content.content:
POWER-TO-WEIGHT GUIDE (kW/kg)
0.04–0.06 = economy/city car feel
0.06–0.10 = family car, adequate for motorways
0.10–0.15 = sporty, confident overtaking
0.15–0.20 = performance car
0.20+ = supercar / track-oriented

SEAT COUNT GUIDE
2 seats = sports car or city two-seater
4 seats = compact/coupe, not a family car
5 seats = standard family baseline
6–7 seats = large family or MPV use; verify 7th seat is a full seat not a child jump seat
8+ seats = minibus / large van configuration
Note: seat count from DMR is KoeretoejOplysningSiddepladserMinimum (minimum configuration). Some models have optional configurations.

INSPECTION OUTCOMES FOR BUYERS
Godkendt = fully approved, no action required
KanGodkendesVedOmsynAf... = conditionally approved — specific defects must be corrected and re-inspected before full approval. Treat as a warning flag when buying used.
IkkeGodkendt = failed inspection — vehicle has uncorrected defects. Significant risk for a used car purchase.
Note: the stored inspection result is the most recent recorded result, not a complete history. A car can be re-inspected.

TOWING SUITABILITY
Braked towing capacity (Tilkoblingsvægt med bremser) is the relevant figure for caravans and trailers with their own brakes.
Typical family caravan: 1200–1800 kg. Horse trailer: 2000–3500 kg. Rule of thumb: towing capacity should be at least 85% of the car's kerbweight for stable towing.

CO2 / EMISSIONS FOR BUYERS
Pre-2017 CO2 figures use NEDC (lower, optimistic). Post-2017 use WLTP (higher, more realistic). Do not compare across the 2017 boundary.
Euro 6 is the current standard. Euro 5 and below face increasing restriction in city low-emission zones.

AWD / 4WD
Traekkendeaksler = 2 means all-wheel drive. 1 means single driven axle (FWD or RWD).
Approx 13.3% of Danish registered passenger cars are AWD.
